MultiprocessTestsg
MultiprocessTests.tearDownc
if 1:
            import sqlite3
            def wait():
                print("started")
                assert "database is locked" in input()
            cx = sqlite3.connect("z
", timeout=a
            cx.create_function("wait", 0, wait)
            with cx:
                cx.execute("create table t(t)")
            try:
                # execute two transactions; both will try to lock the db
                cx.executescript('''
                    -- start a transaction and wait for parent
                    begin transaction;
                    select * from t;
                    select wait();
                    rollback;
                    -- start a new transaction; would fail if parent holds lock
                    begin transaction;
                    select * from t;
                    rollback;
                ''')
            finally:
                cx.close()
        z
utf-8r
encoding
bufsize
stdin
stdoutZ
started)
timeoutz
insert into t values('test')z
no error
end)
inputr^
